verses.text: 'I will bless the LORD at all times,' David resolves, inviting others to magnify the LORD with him. Praise is to be constant and shared. The grateful heart blesses God in every season and draws others to do the same.

'I will bless the LORD at all times,' David resolves, inviting others to magnify the LORD with him. Praise is to be constant and shared. The grateful heart blesses God in every season and draws others to do the same.

He sought the LORD and was heard and delivered from all his fears, for 'the angel of the LORD encampeth round about them that fear him.' God answers and guards His own. Those who reverence Him are encircled by an unseen, mighty protection.

'O taste and see that the LORD is good,' for those who fear and seek Him 'shall not want any good thing.' Faith is an experience, not a theory. Come to God yourself, and you will find Him good beyond report.

'O taste and see that the LORD is good,' for those who fear and seek Him 'shall not want any good thing.' Faith is an experience, not a theory. Come to God yourself, and you will find Him good beyond report.

He teaches the fear of the LORD: whoever would love life must keep his tongue from evil, turn from wrong, and 'seek peace, and pursue it.' Godliness shapes daily speech and conduct. The good life begins with a guarded tongue and a heart bent toward peace.

The eyes of the LORD are on the righteous and His ears open to their cry, but His face is set against evildoers. God watches over both, to bless and to judge. The righteous are seen with favour; the wicked, with reckoning.

The eyes of the LORD are on the righteous and His ears open to their cry, but His face is set against evildoers. God watches over both, to bless and to judge. The righteous are seen with favour; the wicked, with reckoning.
When the righteous cry, the LORD hears and delivers, for 'he is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart.' Brokenness draws Him near. God is closest to His people in their lowest grief.
When the righteous cry, the LORD hears and delivers, for 'he is nigh unto them that are of a broken heart.' Brokenness draws Him near. God is closest to His people in their lowest grief.

'Many are the afflictions of the righteous: but the LORD delivereth him out of them all,' keeping his very bones; He redeems His servants. Trouble is not absent, but it is not the end. God carries His own safely through every affliction.
